2605.13830.title: Quantifying Sensitivity for Tree Ensembles: A symbolic and compositional approach
2605.13830.paper_summary: Decision tree ensembles (DTE) are a popular model for a wide range of AI classification tasks, used in multiple safety critical domains, and hence verifying properties on these models has been an active topic of study over the last decade. One such verification question is the problem of sensitivity, which asks, given a DTE, whether a small change in subset of features can lead to misclassification of the input. In this work, our focus is to build a quantitative notion of sensitivity, tailored to DTEs, by discretizing the input space of the model and enumerating the regions which are susceptible to sensitivity. We propose a novel algorithmic technique that can perform this computation efficiently, within a certified error and confidence bound. Our approach is based on encoding the problem as an algebraic decision diagram (ADD), and further splitting it into subproblems that can be solved efficiently and make the computation compositional and scalable. We evaluate the performance of our technique over benchmarks of varying size in terms of number of trees and depth, comparing it against the performance of model counters over the same problem encoding. Experimental results show that our tool XCount achieves significant speedup over other approaches and can scale well with the increasing sizes of the ensembles.
2605.13830.paper_summary_zh: 決策樹集成（DTE）是廣泛應用於各種人工智慧分類任務的熱門模型，並且在多個安全關鍵領域中使用，因此在過去十年中，驗證這些模型的特性已成為一個活躍的研究主題。這樣的驗證問題之一是敏感性問題，該問題詢問，給定一個 DTE，某些特徵的微小變化是否會導致輸入的錯誤分類。在這項工作中，我們的重點是建立一個量化的敏感性概念，專門針對 DTE，通過離散化模型的輸入空間並列舉出對敏感性易受影響的區域。我們提出了一種新穎的算法技術，可以在經過認證的誤差和置信區間內高效地執行這一計算。我們的方法基於將問題編碼為代數決策圖（ADD），並進一步將其拆分為可以高效解決的子問題，使計算具有可組合性和可擴展性。我們在不同大小的基準測試中評估了我們技術的性能，根據樹的數量和深度進行比較，並將其與相同問題編碼的模型計數器的性能進行比較。實驗結果顯示，我們的工具 XCount 在速度上顯著超越其他方法，並且能夠隨著集成的規模增大而良好擴展。
2605.13830.author: S. Akshay et.al.
2605.13830.authors: S. Akshay, Chaitanya Garg, Ashutosh Gupta, Kuldeep S. Meel, Ajinkya Naik
